summary: Tested Phase A/B/C feature pass (Trust & Security, Walkthrough+SmartDiscovery, Optimizer/Perk Tips). Backend: 5/5 new pytest tests PASS — /api/optimizer/tips returns {tips:[],total:0} for empty wallet; Tata Neu rules tip with kind=redeem/transfer fires; expiry urgent tip fires for voucher expiring in 5 days; full signup/login/me/logout flow works; /api/auth/wipe deletes user-scoped vouchers and removes user account (subsequent /api/auth/me returns 401 'User not found'). Existing backend pytest suite remains intact. Frontend: full first-launch journey works — Auth signup → PinLock set+confirm → 4-slide Walkthrough (eyebrows tracking/growing/family/safe, Skip tour, Continue×3, Get started) → SmartDiscovery (Paste recent SMS + Maybe later; paste 'Flat Rs 150 off on Swiggy! Code SWIGGY150...' → review → Add to wallet succeeded). Home renders with panic-lock-btn. Profile menu (when opened via JS) shows all required entries: Encrypted badge, Perk Tips + Masterclass tag, Security FAQ, Privacy Control, How we protect you. Perk Tips screen shows 'No tips yet' empty state + data-testid=free-vs-pro comparison card. Security FAQ screen renders with first question expanded by default ('Can you read my bank OTPs?'). Privacy Control screen persists 4 prefs in localStorage perk_orbit_privacy_prefs across reload (smsScanEnabled/cameraScanEnabled/notificationsEnabled/pasteSmsExtractionEnabled). HOWEVER — discovered one CRITICAL UX-blocking overlay bug (see below).

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: The floating panic-lock-btn (fixed top-4 right-4, z-50) completely overlaps the profile-avatar (fixed-positioned at top right, ~y=26 x=330, 40x40). document.elementFromPoint at the avatar's visual center returns 'panic-lock-btn'. Result: every tap a user makes on their avatar fires Lock instead of opening the Profile menu. This blocks the user from reaching Perk Tips, Security FAQ, Privacy Control, How-we-protect modal, Settings, Membership, Family Circle, SMS Auto-Scanner, Support History, Privacy Policy, and Logout via the Profile route. Reproduced by tapping the avatar → app navigates to PIN-unlock screen.

Toggles do NOT expose role="switch" / aria-checked, making them invisible to assistive tech and to standard a11y-based testing selectors. Functionality works (localStorage persists), but consider adding role="switch" + aria-checked or using a proper Switch primitive.
FAQ question buttons do not expose aria-expanded — a11y miss. Functionality verified (first question 'Can you read my bank OTPs?' shows expanded, others collapsed with chevrons).

issues: panic-lock-btn (white circle, lock icon) sits directly on top of profile-avatar (green M circle) — visually you can see both, but the lock button shifted +4px right and -12px up creates a confusing dual-control area where the lock dominates the tap target.

action_items: CRITICAL: Move panic-lock-btn so it does NOT overlap profile-avatar. Options: (a) place panic-lock-btn at top-LEFT (top-4 left-4) since the bell+avatar already occupy top-right; (b) move it below the TopBar (e.g. fixed bottom-24 right-4 above BottomNav); or (c) integrate it INTO the TopBar as a sibling button beside the bell, with sufficient gap. Until fixed, end users cannot reach the Profile menu and therefore cannot reach Perk Tips, Security FAQ, Privacy Control, Settings, etc.
Add role="switch" + aria-checked to PrivacyControlScreen toggle buttons (a11y).
Add aria-expanded on SecurityFAQScreen question buttons (a11y).
critical_code_review_comments: App.jsx panic-lock-btn z-index/position collides with TopBar avatar — both `fixed top-4 right-4`. Position one of them differently.
optimizer.py is clean and well-structured; _rule_for() loose-match logic is reasonable. Consider tightening: when key='hdfc' and brand='HDFC Diners' it still matches via substring — fine for now.

auth_intel.wipe_all_data does not delete documents whose user_pin is the legacy short PIN (e.g. '1234') if the account was created with pin_to_claim — it relies on uid matching. Should also wipe by referrer_pin/referee_pin AND by any stored user_pin equal to user['email'] or original pin if persisted. Currently relies on _bind_legacy_pin having migrated them, which is fine if signup was used.
PrivacyControlScreen toggles missing role=switch / aria-checked (a11y miss).
SecurityFAQScreen question buttons missing aria-expanded (a11y miss).

success_rate.frontend: ~85% (all screens render and integrate correctly; one CRITICAL overlay bug blocks the Profile menu via touch)

test_credentials: Brand-new accounts created per run: e2e-{ts}@perkorbit.app / test1234. PIN '1234' set on first launch. Existing cloud account test@perkorbit.app / Perk@1234 also valid.
seed_data_creation: Created throwaway accounts e2e-1781694xxx@perkorbit.app during UI run; wipe-test account deleted itself via /api/auth/wipe. A few TEST_-prefixed vouchers were created and deleted during backend tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-test focus after fix: tap profile-avatar with REAL click (not force=True / not JS .click()) on a fresh mobile viewport (390x844). Verify avatar opens the dropdown menu (Membership, Perk Tips, Family Circle, Settings, Lock app, etc.) instead of triggering Lock. Also re-verify panic-lock-btn still works after relocation. Backend regression: `pytest backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml` should be 23/23 (18 existing + 5 new). For LLM-fallback path in optimizer, pass use_llm_fallback=true and brand='Croma' (unknown brand) — that branch is untested here to avoid LLM latency. Walkthrough only triggers when localStorage 'perk_orbit_tour_done' != '1' AND user just completed PIN set — use a fresh browser context or call page.evaluate localStorage.clear() before navigation.
rca of the issue: PANIC-LOCK OVERLAY BUG ROOT CAUSE: App.jsx renders a global `<button data-testid="panic-lock-btn" className="fixed top-4 right-4 z-50 ...">` near the bottom of the App component. The HomeScreen TopBar also positions the profile avatar at top-right via flex layout, but at the same visual coordinates. document.elementFromPoint(350, 46) returns panic-lock-btn (z-50 wins). Reproduction: 1) Sign up fresh account, 2) Set PIN, 3) Skip tour, 4) Skip discovery, 5) On Home, tap green 'M' avatar in top-right corner → app navigates to PIN-unlock screen ('Welcome back, Enter your PIN'). Mitigation: Move panic-lock-btn to top-left (top-4 left-4) — that corner is empty. Quick fix is a 4-character change in App.jsx where panic-lock-btn className includes 'right-4' → change to 'left-4'.
